steps.title: Recognize the list structure
steps.content: After "namely," the sentence lists three factors: size and composition, velocity, and atmospheric fluctuations. Because later list items include commas, semicolons are the clearest way to separate the items.

steps.title: Reject weaker separators
steps.content: Choice B ends the sentence in the middle of the list. Choice C uses only a comma, which blurs the boundary between list items. Choice D inserts a dash where the sentence needs list punctuation.

steps.title: Separate the first two factors
steps.content: A semicolon after meteoroids cleanly separates the first factor from the second factor, their velocity upon entering Earth's mesosphere. The answer is A.
